item.title: Turkish Oil Tanker Hit by Drones in Black Sea Near Istanbul
item.description: A Turkish tanker laden with crude oil was targeted by a drone attack in the Black Sea near Istanbul, NTV television reported.

item.title: Philippines Suspends Electricity Market to Prevent Price Surge
item.description: The Philippines suspended its wholesale electricity spot market on Thursday, the latest in a series of emergency measures meant to address energy supply risks and price volatility triggered by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Novo Chairman Faces Testy Investors With Little Progress So Far
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S’s Chairman Lars Rebien Sorensen will face investors at the company’s annual meeting for the first time on Thursday after his boardroom coup. He’s in for an earful.

item.title: Korea’s Lee Urges Less Power Use, Driving to Avoid Energy Crunch
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung called on the public to pare back power usage and ride public transport rather than driving cars as his government mounts a national effort to avoid energy shortages should the Iran conflict drag on.

item.title: China’s CXMT Rides AI Boom to Record Revenue Ahead of IPO
item.description: ChangXin Memory Technologies Inc. more than doubled revenue to $8 billion in 2025, giving the strategically important Chinese chipmaker a major boost ahead of one of this year’s biggest domestic IPOs.

item.title: Wheat Spread Hits Seven-Month High as US Plains Crop Fears Grow
item.description: Hard-red winter wheat traded at its biggest premium to the soft red variety in more than seven months, as heat in the central US is set to exacerbate the stress on fields there.

item.title: Foreigners Set to Pull Record Funds From Emerging Asia Stocks
item.description: Global investors are on track to withdraw a record amount from Asian emerging-market equities excluding China, as surging oil prices due to the Middle East conflict clouds the region’s outlook.

item.title: Ex-Thai PM Srettha Wants Debt-Cap Hike to 80% to Revive Growth
item.description: Former Thai Prime Minister Srettha Thavisin called for a rise in the public debt ceiling to both cushion against the effects of the Middle East conflict and revive long-term growth.

item.title: South Korea to Buy Back $3.3 Billion of Bonds to Curb Volatility
item.description: The South Korean government will conduct an emergency buyback of 5 trillion won ($3.3 billion) in sovereign bonds to stabilize markets amid heightened volatility linked to the Iran war.

item.title: More US Counties See Population Drops Under Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: Four in 10 US counties shrank last year as President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown continued to stifle the nation’s main source of population growth.

item.title: Markets Want to Declare an Off-Ramp and Go Home
item.description: Without a deal, the best outcome may be for the US to forcibly reopen the Strait.

item.title: Saudi Oil Sales to Top Asia Buyers to Drop on Hormuz Disruptions
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s oil sales to Asia’s two biggest importers are set to come in at lower-than-usual levels next month, as the war raging across the Middle East disrupts supplies from the energy powerhouse.

item.title: Philippines Receives Russian Oil After US Sanctions Waiver
item.description: Philippine refiner Petron Corp. has received a shipment of Russian oil, according to its chief executive officer, after the US issued a waiver allowing the purchase of the crude.

item.title: Indian Oil Buys First Iranian LPG Since 2018 as Crunch Worsens
item.description: Indian Oil Corp. has bought liquefied petroleum gas from Iran for the first time in almost eight years, according to people familiar with the matter, as the country scrambles to avert a worsening shortage of the vital cooking fuel spurred by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Australian Ore Miner Fenix Warns Fuel Crunch Impacting Industry
item.description: Fenix Resources Ltd. warned that constraints to diesel supply due to the Iran war are starting to impact operations across Australia’s mining sector, forcing the iron ore miner to scale back some activities.

item.title: Copper Falls With Most Metals as Iran War Keeps Traders on Edge
item.description: Copper fell along with other base metals as investors monitored the uncertainty surrounding US-Iran negotiations to possibly end the war.

item.title: Singapore Says China Can Play Bigger Role in Asia’s Stability
item.description: Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong said China will be crucial in anchoring Asia’s growth prospects at a time when the global economy is strained by conflict and trade fragmentation.

item.title: Inflation Pulse From AI and Energy Will Last Decades, IFM Says
item.description: Enormous spending on artificial intelligence and the global energy transition are likely to cause inflationary pressures for decades to come, according to the chief executive of global infrastructure money manager IFM Investors.

item.title: Memory Stock Boom Seen Resilient to Threat From New Google Tech
item.description: Shares of computer memory and storage products slumped on concerns over demand after Google researchers touted a new compression technique. But it may be a hiccup rather than an existential threat.

item.title: Malaysia Plans to Cut Subsidized Fuel Quota, The Edge Says
item.description: Malaysia plans to reduce the monthly subsidized entitlement for its most popular fuel amid a surge in oil prices due to the Middle East conflict, The Edge reported.

item.title: BOK Flags Financial Stability Risks as Iran War Fallout Persists
item.description: The Bank of Korea said the country’s financial system remained broadly stable in March, but warned that escalating Middle East tensions and structural vulnerabilities could amplify risks across markets.

item.title: Pop Mart Extends Record Slide After Analysts Cut Price Targets
item.description: Pop Mart International Group Ltd. shares fell as much as 9.7% after analysts cut price targets and downgraded their outlooks, citing signs of slowing overseas momentum and growing reliance on the company’s Labubu franchise in its full-year results.

item.title: Fuel Prices Jump 22% in Thailand After Anutin Slashed Subsidies
item.description: Millions of motorists across Thailand woke up Thursday to the steepest fuel-price increases in decades after the government moved to rein in subsidies strained by surging global oil costs.

item.title: Australian Gas Shortage Delayed to 2029 on Better Batteries and Grid Investment, AEMO Says
item.description: Better batteries and more investment into the main electricity grid have helped delay a predicted natural gas shortage in Australia by a year.

item.title: Japan Short-Tenor Yields Rise to Decades Highs on Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Japan’s two-year government bond yield climbed to its highest level since 1996, while five-year yields hit a record, as expectations build for a near-term Bank of Japan rate hike.

item.title: Fed Chair Nominee Warsh May Need Five Years to Really Shrink the Central Bank’s Balance Sheet
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Warsh wants to significantly shrink the US central bank’s $6.6 trillion balance sheet. He’ll probably need more than one term to do it, according to a top financial economist.

item.title: Japan Should Send Warships to Secure Hormuz, Ex-Adviser Says
item.description: Japan should consider sending warships to help jointly secure the Strait of Hormuz with other nations and protect both its own vessels and those of other nations — even before a ceasefire, according to a former top national security adviser.

item.title: Iran Drafts Law to Impose Tolls for Transiting Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Iranian parliament is working on a draft bill to charge a fee in exchange for providing security to ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz, according to the semi-official Fars news agency.

item.title: Henkel Is Nearing a Deal for Hair Care Brand Olaplex
item.description: Henkel AG is in advanced talks to buy Olaplex Holdings Inc., the hair-care brand that developed a cult following for its shampoos and other treatments, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Citigroup’s Co-Head of Asia Investment Banking Metzger Departs
item.description: Citigroup Inc.’s Jan Metzger, co-head of investment banking for Asia, has stepped down six months after the firm named a new executive to share the role.
